newsSince: No fresh material company-specific news in the June 9-23 window. IPG's Q1 2026 results (revenue +16.6% to $265.5M, TRUMPF patent dispute settled for ~$13.5M) and Q2 guide ($260-290M, 37-40% adj GM with ~150bp tariff headwind) were disclosed at the early-May earnings call. Management flagged tougher 2H26 comps. Nothing new since June 9.

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent AI supply-chain lens: photonics/precision-manufacturing optionality intact. Q1 beat and TRUMPF settlement already digested pre-window; tougher 2H26 comps flagged. No new June catalyst to move the rank-9 standing on this page.
verdicts.bull: Industrial laser cycle recovering (+16.6% Q1); photonics expertise gives AI-manufacturing optionality; litigation overhang cleared.
verdicts.neutral: Earnings news pre-dates window; nothing new since June 9.
verdicts.bear: Tougher 2H26 comps, ~150bp tariff GM drag, industrial cycle exposure; AI linkage indirect.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Industrial capex recovery and laser-automation demand.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Industrial laser cycle softening into 2H26 against hard comps.

verdicts.rankRationale: Co-packaged-optics lens: IPG still lacks a direct CPO data-center revenue path vs COHR/LITE/MTSI. No new CPO evidence since the June 9 verdict that already moved it down. Industrial-laser identity unchanged. Hold at rank 35.
verdicts.bull: Deep photonics IP could eventually feed CPO/datacom adjacencies.
verdicts.neutral: No CPO design-win or product evidence emerged in the window.
verdicts.bear: Revenue remains industrial-laser-driven; CPO path far less direct than pure datacom optics peers.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: A concrete CPO/datacom photonics revenue path.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Stays a peripheral name on a pure-CPO lens.
